Title: MACC chief denies political interference in investigations
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki has reiterated that no political interference or directives have ever influenced the agency's investigations.
He said the MACC does not accept such instructions as its operations are overseen by five independent monitoring bodies.
Azam said the primary challenge facing the commission today is securing solid evidence through intelligence-based investigation (IBI) methods.
"Throughout my leadership of the MACC, there have been no political instructions influencing our probes. For everyone's information, the MACC is also monitored by five independent bodies.
"So, if anyone claims we are not transparent, that is merely the perception of those uncomfortable with our firmness," he said during a simulated press conference featured in the commission's new corporate video launched last night.
MACC Strategic Communications Division director Hisyam Mohd Yusoff said the 12-minute video utilises a storytelling concept with dramatic elements, including acting and plot, to deliver anti-corruption messages in an accessible and relatable manner.
He said Azam and several senior MACC officers make appearances in the video to provide a clear picture of the agency's role, initiatives, and commitment to combating graft.
"This video is our effort to introduce the commission's new face to the community.
"It was produced in two versions, a five-minute short version and a full 13-minute version, complete with English subtitles to expand its international reach," he said.
Hisyam added that the corporate video is expected to serve as an effective medium to showcase the MACC's new image while strengthening public confidence in its ongoing fight against corruption.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Petronas to negotiate oil purchase with Russia to secure national supply
Article:
KOTA BARU: Petroliam Nasional Berhad (Petronas) will negotiate with Russia to purchase oil to ensure the country maintains sufficient fuel supplies.
Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said many countries in Europe and the United States, which previously sanctioned Russia, were now competing to purchase oil from the nation.
"Fortunately, our relations with Russia remain good, allowing the Petronas team to negotiate with them," he said after officiating the new Sultan Ismail Petra Airport (LTSIP) terminal here today.
Also present were Transport Minister Anthony Loke and Kelantan Menteri Besar Datuk Mohd Nassuruddin Daud.
Anwar assured that the national petrol supply is currently secured until June.
He also revealed that his Australian counterpart, Anthony Albanese, who visited Malaysia two days ago, had sought cooperation regarding petrol, diesel, and fertilisers.
"My answer was that we can help friendly nations, but our priority is our own domestic needs. If there is a surplus, we will discuss assisting. If not, we cannot.
"Why must we maintain good relations with Australia? It is because we need their phosphate for our plantations, especially Felda-owned oil palm estates.
"We also have oil processing plants in Australia. This is why international relations must be maintained and negotiated effectively," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: KTMB expands EMUPlus
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Keretapi Tanah Melayu Bhd (KTMB) has expanded its EMUPlus service to Klang, giving Klang Valley residents a more convenient, comfortable and practical direct journey to Ipoh starting this week.
KTMB Group chief executive officer Datuk Azlan Shah Al Bakri said the route includes stops at Klang, Shah Alam, Subang Jaya, KL Sentral and Ipoh.
"Commuters in Klang, Shah Alam and Subang Jaya can now board the EMUPlus closer to their areas, without having to rely entirely on major stations like KL Sentral," he said.
He added that tickets for travel from April 17 to 19 are available, with fares starting as low as RM45 one-way, offering better value and savings.
To encourage more ridership, Azlan Shah said the EMUPlus will operate every weekend, during long holidays and festive seasons, subject to operational needs and planning.
"The introduction of this new access reflects KTMB's commitment to bringing EMUPlus services closer to the people, while strengthening intercity connectivity and enriching the overall rail travel experience," he said.
More information on schedules and ticket purchases can be obtained via the KITS Style app, KTMB's official website, or its social media channels.
– Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: No fuel surcharge for Penang ferry services
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: The Penang Port Commission (SPPP) and Penang Port Sdn Bhd have decided against imposing a fuel surcharge on ferry services for the Pangkalan Raja Tun Uda (PRTU) and Pangkalan Sultan Abdul Halim (PSAH) routes.
SPPP chairman Datuk Yeoh Soon Hin said today that while global energy market uncertainty and geopolitical developments in West Asia had driven up marine diesel prices, the surcharge would not be passed on to users.
He said both SPPP and Penang Port remained committed to balancing operational sustainability with public interest.
"This includes strengthening operational efficiency and managing cost pressures prudently without shifting the burden to the people.
"SPPP will continue to play its role as a trade facilitator and port regulator by ensuring ferry services remain sustainable and resilient while supporting mobility and economic activities in Penang," he said.
Yeoh added that ferry services were a vital component of the national maritime transport system, which relies significantly on fuel.
Although the government introduced a nationwide fuel surcharge mechanism on April 20 as a mitigation measure, he confirmed that it would not apply to the two main Penang routes.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PM attends Pasir Puteh development meeting
Article:
PASIR PUTEH: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim attended the 2026 Pasir Puteh district development meeting at the Pasir Puteh Land and District Office here today.
Anwar, who is also the finance minister, arrived at 11.50am and was greeted by Kelantan Menteri Besar Datuk Mohd Nassuruddin Daud, together with several state executive councillors and senior state officials.
The special meeting was also attended by Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar and Public Service Department director-general Tan Sri Wan Ahmad Dahlan Abdul Aziz.
After the meeting, Anwar is scheduled to attend a meet-and-greet and visit Masjid Gong Chapa here at 1.30pm.
– Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: New Jakarta, Bangkok routes planned for Sultan Ismail Petra Airport, says Loke
Article:
KOTA BARU: The government is looking to attract airlines to introduce new flight routes, including Jakarta and Bangkok, at Sultan Ismail Petra Airport to boost connectivity and draw more travellers, including those from southern Thailand.
Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the initiative marks a new chapter for the airport, which, although classified as a domestic facility, is now capable of handling international flights following the RM440 million recent upgrade.
"In the near future, the government plans to attract airlines to introduce new flight routes at the airport.
"By doing this, we hope this airport can serve as a gateway to the east coast, particularly Kelantan, while also attracting more users from southern Thailand to use Kota Baru Airport as a transit point to destinations such as Bangkok, Jakarta, Kuala Lumpur and Singapore," he said.
He was speaking to reporters after the opening of the airport's new terminal by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today.
Loke said the airport's capacity has been significantly increased from 1.5 million to four million passengers, nearly doubling its previous capability.
He added that efforts would be intensified to attract airlines to position the airport as a hub for the east coast.
"We will engage with airlines and hope our carriers, including Malaysia Airlines and Batik Air, will increase flight frequencies.
"At the same time, we welcome more international airlines to operate here," he said.
He the airport is now equipped with immigration and customs facilities, enabling it to handle international flights.
"It has the potential to become a key gateway. Our focus is not only on serving the east coast population but also on attracting travellers from southern Thailand, including areas like Golok.
"For them, travelling to other airports is much farther, so we hope this will draw more users.
"With better connectivity, more people will come to Kota Baru and Kelantan, which will in turn support the tourism sector," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Elderly man killed in crash while heading for Friday prayers
Article:
KAJANG: A 79-year-old man on his way to a mosque for Friday prayers with his grandson was killed after his motorcycle collided with a lorry in Jalan Hulu Langat yesterday.
District police chief Assistant Commissioner Naazron Abdul Yusof said police received a report involving the Honda EX5 motorcycle and the lorry at 1.20pm.
"Investigations found that the accident occurred when the motorcyclist attempted to cross the road in front of the lorry, which was travelling straight.
"The lorry driver was unaware of the motorcycle and struck the victim. The elderly man was confirmed dead at the scene," he said in a statement today.
His 14-year-old grandson who was riding pillion and the 29-year-old lorry driver escaped unhurt.
The incident gained attention earlier on social media after photos of the crash went viral, with netizens noting the victim was heading to perform his Friday prayers.
Naazron said the case is being investigated under Section 41(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987.
He urged anyone with information to contact investigating officer Inspector Nur Faezah Mohd Zakaria at 013-3268497.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Search underway for student feared drowned at Pantai Balok
Article:
KUANTAN: The Pahang Education Department is monitoring a case involving a 16-year-old student who is feared drowned after going missing while foraging for clams at Pantai Balok here yesterday evening.
The department confirmed the victim was among several students who had left their hostel for recreational activities without the school's knowledge.
In a statement today, the department clarified that the outing was not part of an official school programme and expressed hope for the student's safe recovery.
It was earlier reported that the Pahang Fire and Rescue Department received an emergency call at 6.02pm.
Assistant operations director Mohd Salahuddin Isa said a team from the Kuantan Fire and Rescue Station was deployed to the scene and began search operations along the shoreline with the assistance of the public.
"The victim has yet to be found and the search and rescue (SAR) operation is ongoing," he said in a statement yesterday.
Mohd Salahuddin said the mission involves 12 personnel, a Fire Rescue Tender (FRT), a four-wheel-drive vehicle, a lorry, and an Emergency Medical Rescue Services (EMRS) unit.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: HRD Corp reset aims to boost governance, execution and accountability, says CEO
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Human Resource Development Corporation (HRD Corp) has completed a sweeping organisational reset, marking a decisive shift towards execution, governance and delivery as it seeks to rebuild public confidence and strengthen Malaysia's workforce development ecosystem.
The reset, carried out over the past two months, involved a full restructuring across governance, management and operations aimed at addressing longstanding weaknesses flagged in previous audits and regulatory reviews.
HRD Corp chief executive Datuk Shamir Aziz said the organisation is now firmly aligned with the policy direction set by Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan, particularly in reinforcing governance standards and ensuring training systems remain credible and industry-relevant.
"This reflects the Madani principle through execution, where integrity, accountability and discipline are enforced in practice, not merely stated.
"The reset is done. We move forward with discipline, clarity and execution," he said.
He said the core of the reset was the resolution of legacy issues stemming from earlier audit findings, including weaknesses in oversight, compliance gaps and procedural shortcomings in the management of training claims and levy utilisation.
"Previously, companies were allowed to submit claims after training had been conducted — in some cases even on the same day — limiting HRD Corp's ability to verify and monitor activities effectively.
"Under newly tightened standard operating procedures, training programmes must now receive prior approval within a defined timeframe before implementation. This enables enforcement teams to be deployed on the ground, ensuring better visibility, compliance and accountability," Shamir said in a statement today.
He added that restructuring also involved a comprehensive realignment of personnel based on capability and function, alongside the strengthening of critical departments such as legal, audit and enforcement to reinforce institutional integrity.
"Operational structures have been streamlined to improve execution, while strategic functions have been recalibrated to sharpen focus and accountability across the organisation."
In a move to close leadership gaps, Shamir said HRD Corp has appointed a chief financial officer and chief operating officer, positions that were previously vacant, and has promoted internal talent into key management roles to ensure continuity and performance.
"The agency has also completed its forward budget planning, which has been approved by its board of directors. The plan includes a significant allocation towards national workforce development priorities, with further details expected to be announced," he said.
Shamir stressed that the organisation is determined to move beyond past shortcomings and deliver tangible results.
"Our responsibility now is execution, to deliver results, restore confidence and ensure Malaysian workers and businesses are equipped to withstand and respond to increasing global uncertainties.
"Moving forward, stakeholders can expect stricter oversight of training funds, improved transparency in levy utilisation and more robust enforcement of compliance standards."
With governance structures strengthened and leadership reinforced, HRD Corp is positioning itself to play a more decisive role in building a resilient, future-ready workforce amid challenging global uncertainties.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Lorry driver held in Klang for smuggling 3,400 litres of diesel
Article:
KLANG: Selangor Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry enforcement officers foiled an attempt by a lorry driver to flee with 3,400 litres of subsidised diesel early today.
State ministry chief enforcement officer Mohd Hanizam Kechek said a driver in his 20s was detained along with a bonded lorry modified with an additional fuel tank capacity of 6,000 litres.
"Patrols under Op Tiris 4.0 detected the suspicious vehicle filling up at a petrol station in Pandamaran.
"An inspection revealed six Intermediate Bulk Container (IBC) units, each with a 1,000-litre capacity, filled with fuel suspected to be diesel.
"The lorry body had been modified with transfer equipment, including connecting hoses and metered pumps," he said in a statement today.
Hanizam said the suspect is believed to have filled up at several stations in the area based on purchase receipts found in the vehicle.
Further checks revealed the suspect did not possess valid documents to carry or own the subsidised fuel.
"Action is also being taken against the petrol station operator under the Control of Supplies Regulations 1974," he said.
The total value of the seizure is estimated at over RM100,000.
Hanizam noted that the prompt intervention saved approximately RM20,000 in government subsidies in this case alone.
He warned that the ministry would not hesitate to recommend the cancellation or suspension of Subsidised Diesel Control Scheme (SKDS) fleet cards for those found misappropriating fuel.
The public can report similar activities via WhatsApp at 019-848 8000, the Ez Adu KPDN app, the ministry's hotline at 1-800-886-800, or its e-complaint portal at http://eduan.kpdn.gov.my.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Petronas vessels among Malaysian ships awaiting Strait of Hormuz passage, says PM
Article:
KOTA BARU: The remaining four Malaysian vessels are expected to take time before being allowed to pass through the Strait of Hormuz following the latest developments, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today.
He confirmed that vessels belonging to Petronas were among the earliest to be granted passage through the strategic waterway amid ongoing regional tensions.
He said one Petronas vessel had already arrived in Pengerang, while another was nearing Malaysian waters.
"One ship has arrived in Pengerang, Johor, and another is close to entering Malaysian waters, making it two so far.
"The others, although granted approval, are still being monitored," he said at the opening of the new Sultan Ismail Petra Airport terminal here today.
he said Iran had approved passage for seven Malaysian vessels, but one could not depart due to technical issues at the port.
"Due to the latest developments, we are facing some constraints. At present, we are closely monitoring four more vessels that have yet to pass through the Strait of Hormuz," he said.
He said the situation in the strategic waterway has implications for transportation and could affect Malaysia, but dismissed claims that Malaysian vessels were unable to pass through.
"Some have said our ships are unable to pass, but a Petronas vessel reached Pengerang yesterday.
"We are grateful that early engagement, including direct contact with the Iranian leadership, enabled at least one vessel to pass through initially.
"Four vessels are still pending, and although approvals have been given, the process will take time.
"This is a challenge faced globally," he said.
He said Malaysia remains open to assisting friendly nations, but priority would be given to domestic needs.
"Our focus is on our country first. If there is capacity, we will extend assistance to others," he said.
He stressed the importance of maintaining strong diplomatic ties with various countries to ensure cooperation during such crises.
He added that Malaysia's international relations remain intact, enabling Petronas to continue negotiations effectively despite the challenging environment.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ministry detects 31 cases of foreign vehicles filling RON95 since April 1st
Article:
JOHOR BARU: The Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry (KPDN) has detected 31 cases of foreign-registered vehicles filling up with RON95 petrol nationwide since the ban took effect on April 1.
Enforcement director-general Datuk Azman Adam said the lower fuel price in Malaysia compared with neighbouring countries was among the factors driving the offences.
"Some also claimed they were unaware of which petrol stations sell RON97," he said after joining an integrated operation with the Road Transport Department (JPJ) here last night.
Also present was JPJ director-general Datuk Aedy Fadly Ramli.
Azman said offenders could face compounds or court action, with fines of up to RM1 million upon conviction, depending on the type of offence and amount involved.
He added that several petrol station operators were still configuring their systems for the full implementation of the ban on the use of foreign credit and debit cards at self-service fuel terminals.
Meanwhile, Aedy Fadly said JPJ had issued 5,984 summons notices involving compounds totalling RM2.195 million under Vehicle Entry Permit (VEP) enforcement since July 1, 2025.
A total of 82,987 vehicles were inspected at Bangunan Sultan Iskandar (BSI), Kompleks Sultan Abu Bakar (KSAB) and Taman Daya here, he said.
He added that JPJ and KPDN were carrying out nationwide enforcement, including in Johor, to monitor foreign-registered vehicles and ensure compliance with laws governing subsidised goods.
The cooperation also includes integration of the VEP system and Automatic Number Plate Recognition (ANPR), enabling real-time detection of foreign vehicles and the sharing of owner profiles and travel records between agencies to improve enforcement efficiency and curb subsidy abuse. — BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Multi-agency retreat to tackle online scams soon, says Fahmi
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A multi-agency retreat to address online scams will be held in the near future to streamline legal, enforcement and coordination efforts across government bodies.
Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the initiative by the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) aims to address gaps in understanding and coordination among agencies, particularly in tackling increasingly complex online fraud cases.
He said this follows the Strategic Coordination Meeting on Addressing Scam Issues held on April 9 between MCMC, Bukit Aman's Commercial Crime Investigation Department and Criminal Investigation Department, as well as key industry stakeholders.
"Sometimes, issues seen by one agency are not fully understood by another. So at MCMC, we have taken a proactive step by bringing together all parties involved in fraud and online scam cases.
"This is not limited to a single category, but includes investment scams, love scams and various other crimes involving the misuse of network services," he told reporters this after launching the Universiti Malaya Public Health Awareness Campaign in Pantai Dalam today.
He said the retreat would involve telecommunications companies, banking institutions and industry associations, including the Association of Banks in Malaysia (ABM) and the Association of Islamic Banking and Financial Institutions Malaysia (AIBIM).
He said discussions would focus on identifying key challenges, including legal frameworks, enforcement mechanisms and the need for more integrated cross-agency action.
He also said the initiative would cover all types of scams, including investment scams, love scams and job scams, which often exploit digital communication platforms.
"These scams are not limited to social media. They also take place via messaging platforms such as WhatsApp and Telegram, which are widely used for communication," he said.
Fahmi said the retreat, expected to take place within the next few weeks, would also look into whether amendments to existing laws are necessary.
He added that the outcome is expected to strengthen coordinated efforts among ministries, departments and agencies in tackling online fraud more effectively.
Earlier, Berita Harian reported that loan shark syndicates are becoming increasingly aggressive and harder to detect as they shift towards the use of digital platforms and social media, posing a serious threat to the public.
Bukit Aman Commercial Crime Investigation Department director Datuk Rusdi Mohd Isa was reported as saying that this tactic enables syndicates to target vulnerable groups through online advertisements.
Over the five-year period from 2021 to last year, a total of 6,277 cases were recorded nationwide, with total losses amounting to RM28.28 million.
Of this total, 1,000 cases were recorded in 2021, increasing to 1,069 cases in 2022, 1,186 cases in 2023 and 1,071 cases in 2024, before rising sharply to 2,011 cases in 2025.
Victim losses were recorded at RM4.02 million in 2021, increasing to RM6.96 million in 2022, RM5.93 million in 2023, RM5.30 million in 2024 and RM6.05 million in 2025.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Proposal to retain 300-litre RON95 quota for media to be discussed, says Fahmi
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A proposal to retain the 300-litre monthly Budi Madani RON95 (Budi95) fuel quota for media practitioners will be tabled at the National Economic Action Council (MTEN) meeting next Tuesday.
Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the proposal, raised by the Gabungan Kelab Media Malaysia (GKMM), would be deliberated at the meeting, with a decision to be conveyed thereafter.
"I will bring the matter to the MTEN meeting next Tuesday for further consideration," he said after launching the Universiti Malaya Public Health Awareness Campaign in Pantai Dalam here today.
Yesterday, GKMM urged the government to consider a special exemption for registered media practitioners to maintain the 300-litre monthly RON95 quota.
The group said it had received numerous complaints from media personnel, particularly those based outside the Klang Valley, who are required to travel long distances and commute frequently for daily assignments.
According to GKMM, the government's decision to reduce the Budi95 quota to 200 litres per month has begun to pose significant challenges for media practitioners in carrying out their field duties.
Fahmi said the Communications Ministry is also taking proactive steps to improve access to information for media practitioners, particularly on current issues such as the global energy crisis.
He said that starting next Wednesday, briefings on the global energy crisis, as well as post-cabinet press conferences, will be conducted in a hybrid format.
"This will allow journalists based in Putrajaya to attend physically, while those in Kuala Lumpur can participate online and pose questions," he said.
Fahmi, who is also unity government spokesman, said the initiative is aimed at ensuring more comprehensive access to information while taking into account logistical and cost constraints faced by media practitioners.
"As the unity government spokesman, I see the need to facilitate media access to information directly, regardless of location."
Effective April 1, the monthly Budi95 quota was adjusted to 200 litres from 300 litres
following ongoing tensions in the Middle East, although the subsidised RON95 price remains at RM1.99 per litre.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Refurbished Sultan Ismail Petra Airport to boost Asean links, says PM
Article:
KOTA BARU: The refurbished Sultan Ismail Petra Airport (LTSIP) in Pengkalan Chepa is set to open direct links to key Asean hubs, boosting Kelantan's trade and tourism, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said.
He said the RM440 million airport would serve as a major economic catalyst for the state while strengthening its connectivity to the regional network.
"LTSIP, a RM440 million airport, is set to become an economic catalyst and connect Kelantan to the regional network," he said at the opening ceremony.
He also praised the airport's design and overall upgrade, describing it as a standout example of infrastructure that blends modernity with cultural identity.
"It is an example of infrastructure that combines modern technology with cultural elements. I am impressed by the airport's transformation," he said, comparing it with other facilities he had visited.
"I would like to thank the Transport Ministry and the airport authorities because this airport… when I came last year, it was truly remarkable, among the most beautiful, modern, and with artistic elements," he said.
He highlighted the incorporation of Islamic and Malay artistic features in the airport.
"If you want to see an airport that reflects Islamic and Malay artistic heritage, come to Kota Baru," he said.
He said development must go beyond physical improvements and reflect cultural depth.
"Beyond physical development that provides convenience, it must also offer what we call character," he said.
Drawing a comparison with global cities, Anwar recounted the observation of a thinker visiting Washington.
"He said the buildings were new and technologically advanced, but culturally lacking, beautiful structures, but with a sense of cultural emptiness.
"In contrast, when you come to Kota Baru, especially this airport, it is modern, equipped with new technology, and its cultural identity is evident."
The upgraded LTSIP features expanded terminal capacity, improved passenger amenities, and design elements inspired by local heritage, positioning it as a key gateway to the east coast.
Also present were Transport Minister Anthony Loke, Kelantan Menteri Besar Datuk Mohd Nassuruddin Daud and officials from Malaysia Airports Holdings Bhd.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar: Diesel price hike affects 15pct, 85pct still on subsidy
Article:
KOTA BARU: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today acknowledged that about 15 per cent of the population has been affected by recent diesel price increases following the Middle East conflict, stressing that the government is doing its best to ease the burden.
He said while a segment of the people continues to feel the effects, the remaining 85 per cent are still purchasing diesel at subsidised rates rather than market prices.
"People raise the issue of high diesel prices; I acknowledge and agree that some are affected by the adjustment, including those in the transport sector.
"However, only 15 per cent are buying at market prices, while 85 per cent are still buying at subsidised rates," he said after officiating the new Sultan Ismail Petra Airport (LTSIP) terminal in Pengkalan Chepa today.
Also present were Transport Minister Anthony Loke and Kelantan Menteri Besar Datuk Mohd Nassuruddin Daud.
Anwar said the government had implemented various exemptions and targeted measures to cushion the impact, particularly for key sectors and vulnerable groups.
He noted that some had claimed fishermen were adversely affected by the price hike, despite the group still having access to subsidised fuel.
"We have provided many exemptions, but we should not sensationalise isolated cases. I do not deny that there are those affected," he said.
"So, do not use examples like that. Do we need measures to ease the burden on the people? Yes, and we hold discussions every day to find ways to alleviate those burdens," he said.
Anwar added that the government was making daily efforts to provide relief, although global economic pressures continued to limit its capacity.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: UN: Last year was deadliest for Rohingya refugees
Article:
GENEVA, (Switzerland): Last year was the deadliest ever for Rohingya refugees fleeing by boat from Bangladesh or Myanmar across the Indian Ocean towards Indonesia and Malaysia, said the United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) on Friday, reported German Press Agency (dpa).
Approximately 900 people from the ethnic group died or are listed as missing in the Andaman Sea or the Bay of Bengal, the UNHCR said in Geneva.
"More than 6,500 Rohingya attempted perilous sea crossings that year, with one in seven reported missing or dead — the highest mortality rate worldwide of any major route for refugee and migrant sea journeys," it said.
This trend has continued this year as well, with more than 2,800 Rohingya undertaking dangerous sea journeys since January.
Recently, an overcrowded boat carrying an estimated 250 people sank in the Andaman Sea after reportedly leaving Bangladesh for Malaysia at the end of March.
The exact time of the sinking remains unclear. Just nine people were rescued from the water, the UNHCR said.
According to the International Organisation for Migration (IOM), in addition to Rohingya refugees, there were also people from Bangladesh on board.
More than 1.3 million Rohingya refugees currently live in Bangladesh, mostly in refugee camps.
According to UNHCR, this constitutes the world's largest concentration of refugees in camp conditions.
UN agencies report that smugglers and human traffickers are exploiting the precarious situation of the refugees, exposing both Rohingya and Bangladeshi nationals to great danger.
Nine years ago, the military in Myanmar launched an offensive against the Muslim Rohingya minority living there, displacing hundreds of thousands of people. — BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Cabinet ready for pay cuts, says Fahmi
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The cabinet is prepared for further ministerial salary cuts as the government weighs measures to cushion the impact of escalating Middle East tensions on the economy.
Unity government spokesman Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the matter is not new and has been discussed since the administration first took office.
He said cabinet members are fully aware of the issue, adding that any decision ultimately rests with Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
"We have discussed this before, but it is up to the prime minister on what he wants us to implement," he told reporters after launching the Universiti Malaya Public Health Awareness Campaign (PHAC) 2026 here today.
In 2022, the unity government cabinet agreed to a 20 per cent
monthly salary cut
just three days after the Cabinet line-up was announced.
Fahmi, who is also communications minister, said ministers are ready to comply with any directive issued by the prime minister.
"I am prepared to accept any instruction from the prime minister. In fact, since the first day we assumed office, our salaries had already been reduced at that time."
His remarks come after Anwar yesterday said the government may consider reducing
ministers' salaries
if current economic uncertainties persist.
The prime minister said there were no obstacles to such a move, but stressed that performance remained the priority.
"Our ministers' salaries are relatively low. I have no problem — I do not take a salary.
"Salary is their entitlement, as long as they carry out their duties well," he said.
Indonesia is also reportedly considering cutting ministers' salaries as part of fiscal tightening measures.
According to international reports, Cabinet Secretary Teddy Indra Wijaya said the proposal remains under discussion, with no decision made so far.
Meanwhile, Indonesia's Finance Minister Purbaya Yudhi Sadewa previously said he would not oppose such a move, noting that any decision ultimately rests with President Prabowo Subianto.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Economist: Strait of Hormuz reopening good news but too early to celebrate
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Iran's move to reopen the Strait of Hormuz is welcome news, but it is too early to celebrate as the security situation remains fragile with elevated insurance costs, University of Nottingham Malaysia assistant professor of business economics Dr Tan Chee Meng said.
"Shipping volumes through this critical energy chokepoint remain subdued, reflecting the fact that the security situation is still fragile.
"As long as the conflict remains volatile, many shipping firms will judge it too risky to commit vessels to the route without clearer and more durable assurances that hostilities will not flare up again," Tan explained.
The last thing operators want is to dispatch tankers through the strait only to see it closed or disrupted again before they can complete their journeys, he told Bernama when asked to comment on the latest development.
Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi on Friday declared the strait "completely open" for all commercial vessels, linking it to the ceasefire in Lebanon.
The decision was made "in line with the ceasefire in Lebanon", Araghchi said in a statement on the US social media platform X.
A day earlier, United States President Donald Trump had announced a 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon.
Tan said a more immediate constraint on traffic is the cost and availability of insurance.
During the US-Israel war on Iran, war‑risk premiums and deductibles surged, making transiting the strait significantly more expensive.
Assume that a US$500,000 cargo is insured with a deductible of US$5,000. If the cargo sustains US$50,000 in damage, the insurance firm will only pay US$45,000 in compensation, while the remaining US$5,000 is borne by the insured.
However, with the heightened risk, this ratio changes drastically to the point where it is no longer economically viable for both the shipper and insurance firms.
"Those elevated insurance costs have not fallen in line with the reopening, and many shipowners are unwilling to absorb or pass on these additional costs, which helps explain why traffic has been slow to return," he said.
Even with the recent easing in crude prices, a rapid return to pre‑war conditions is unlikely, said Tan.
"Key parts of Iran's energy‑producing and export infrastructure have been damaged, and repairs and reconstruction will take many months, if not longer.
"Until that capacity is restored and supply normalises, oil markets are likely to remain tight and prices relatively elevated," he added. — BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: High profit margins drive 'mobile store' diesel smuggling tactics
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Fuel smuggling syndicates have adopted a new modus operandi by using large tanker lorries as mobile storage units to consolidate subsidised diesel collected by smaller vehicles.
These syndicates deploy dozens of small lorries, known as 'lori semut', to abuse the Subsidised Diesel Control Scheme (SKDS) fleet cards at petrol stations.
The subsidised diesel, initially filled into one to three-tonne lorries, is then transferred into large tankers with capacities ranging from 19,000 to 34,000 litres.
It is believed that this mobile storage tactic has replaced static bases to avoid detection by the authorities. The collected diesel is eventually sold to domestic industries or smuggled abroad using merchant ships.
The activity has intensified following diesel price hikes triggered by Middle East tensions starting Feb 28.
A significant price gap is driving the trend. Subsidised diesel is priced as low as RM1.65 per litre, while market prices reached RM5.97 yesterday and as high as RM6.72 earlier this month.
With a profit margin of RM4 to RM5 per litre, syndicates can generate a net profit of RM15,000 daily if they collect just 3,000 litres. This remains lucrative even after paying drivers, wholesalers, and complicit petrol station operators.
The Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry's enforcement division identified five common tactics used by these syndicates.
Apart from repeated purchases at stations, syndicates use proxy vehicles to move fuel to illegal stores and often use fake local registration numbers on foreign vehicles. They also manipulate identities by using third-party identification cards or purchasing more than 20 litres in external containers without special permits.
The ministry said high demand and global market price hikes caused by the Middle East crisis have pushed syndicates to focus on cheaper subsidised fuel.
These activities are masterminded by smuggling syndicates, companies, individuals, and ineligible industrial players.
The tactic was recently uncovered during a joint operation by the Selangor Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry and the Selangor Police Special Branch in Shah Alam, where a container lorry modified to carry 18,000 litres was intercepted.
A driver and five men were detained, and 21,000 litres of diesel worth RM60,690 were seized.
In a separate case on Sunday, the Penang Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry crippled a syndicate in Butterworth under Ops Tiris 3.0 and Ops Jeriji.
Officers discovered a site used for tank-to-tank transfers, seizing 21,190 litres of diesel with a total value of RM327,963.80. Two Indian nationals in their 20s were detained.
Both cases are being investigated under Section 21 of the Control of Supplies Act 1961. Individual offenders face a fine of up to RM1 million or three years' imprisonment, while subsequent offences carry a fine of up to RM3 million.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'Look at each other' philosophy: A model for shared prosperity
Article:
It has been a year and a half since i assumed my role as Japan's ambassador to Malaysia, and I continue to be impressed by the country's vitality, cultural diversity, and deep affinity with Japan.
Walking through local markets during Ramadan, savouring nasi lemak, mee goreng, and local delicacies, and interacting with students and business leaders reminds me daily of the human connections that form the foundation of our bilateral relationship.
Japan's engagement with Malaysia spans nearly 70 years, during which our nations have developed Malaysia's human and industrial capital.
Under the Look East Policy, approximately 28,000 Malaysian students and trainees studied in Japan, gaining skills in engineering, technology, and management. Many returned to strengthen Malaysia's industrial and technological sectors.
Today, institutions like UTM's Malaysia-Japan International Institute of Technology (MJIIT) continue this legacy, producing graduates with industry-ready skills in semiconductors, AI, and engineering.
The University of Tsukuba Malaysia (UTMy), now in its second year, is preparing the next generation of engineers and researchers who will contribute to Malaysia's innovation ecosystem.
Together, these initiatives exemplify human capital development supporting shared industrial growth.
Complementing talent development is substantial Japanese corporate investment. Around 1,600 Japanese companies operate across manufacturing, technology, and services, with many expanding locally across Malaysia.
From semiconductors and electronics to green technologies, these investments create good jobs, strengthen industrial ecosystems, and reinforce Malaysia as a reliable partner for Japanese industry.
Under Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi, Japan's 17 Growth Strategy sectors provide a roadmap for collaboration, including AI, semiconductors, quantum technology, cybersecurity, green transformation, biotechnology, aerospace, sustainable energy, and more.
These sectors offer tangible benefits for Malaysia:
* Semiconductors & AI: Penang has become a global hub for semiconductor back-end processes, with major Japanese firms such as Renesas Electronics. NTT DATA has been stepping up investments in data centres in Johor and Cyberjaya, supporting AI, cloud, and digital services.
* Green Transformation (GX): Malaysia supplies 15 per cent of Japan's LNG, and Sarawak's hydropower resources are ideal for green hydrogen, ammonia, and CCUS projects.
CHITOSE launched Carbon Capture Central, the world's largest mass microalgae production facility in Sarawak. Euglena, in partnership with Petronas, is pioneering a major joint venture biorefinery project in Pengerang, Johor, to produce sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) initiatives, combining Malaysian renewable resources with Japanese technology.
* Food Technology & Biotechnology: Malaysia's globally trusted halal certification enables exports worldwide. Japanese firms such as Ajinomoto Malaysia already export halal-certified products to the Middle East and Japan.
Malaysia's tropical agriculture offers opportunities for smart farming and bioresource research, fostering joint innovation. Examples include strawberry farming in Cameron Highlands, where advanced cultivation techniques are applied; CHITOSE's algae production in Sarawak, demonstrating how Malaysian bioresources and Japanese technology can create next-generation, sustainable food and biotech products; and Euglena's microalgae projects with Malaysian partners that explore cultivation and downstream applications spanning nutrition, functional ingredients, and sustainable materials.
* Content & Digital Industries: Japanese anime, manga, and games are hugely popular in Malaysia, positioning the country as a regional content hub for Asean. Building on this momentum, Kadokawa's collaboration with Malaysia's Gempak Starz is expanding the co-creation and distribution of IP, while the new K+ store at LaLaport BBCC showcases Japanese and Malaysian titles side by side—including Malaysian fan-favourite IP such as Lawak Kampus.
To deepen the creative talent pipeline, Nippon Designers School (NDS) Malaysia Campus — launched in 2022 — has already graduated its first cohort, contributing skilled designers and creators to Malaysia's growing digital and content industries.
Other sectors, including pharmaceuticals, advanced medicine, aerospace, fusion energy, disaster resilience, logistics, maritime technologies, and defence, also offer opportunities for industrial upgrading, talent development, and technological collaboration.
These initiatives were further explored at the New Japan-Malaysia Industrial Cooperation Seminar on March 31, 2026, where leaders from both governments and the private sector discussed concrete investment pathways, pilot projects, joint ventures, and talent development programmes.
For those interested in learning more, the archived video of the seminar is available on our Facebook page: https://www.facebook.com/JapaninMalaysia/
While the Look East Policy focused primarily on Malaysia learning from Japan, today our relationship emphasises mutual exchange and co-creation, a philosophy i describe as "Look at Each Other."
Japanese students are increasingly enrolling in Malaysian universities, participating in research, and contributing to Malaysia's innovation ecosystem.
Combined with Malaysian talent, digital infrastructure, and strategic resources, Japanese investment and technology allow us to co-develop industries that benefit both nations.
As we approach the 70th anniversary of diplomatic relations in 2027, the Japan-Malaysia partnership has established a strong foothold in human capital development, industrial cooperation, and shared innovation.
By moving from "Look East" to "Look at Each Other," both nations are poised to achieve shared prosperity, build sustainable industries, and serve as a model for innovation, technology, and green growth across Asean.
The writer is Ambassador of Japan to Malaysia
The views expressed in this article are the author's own and do not necessarily reflect those of the New Straits Times
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Trump says Iran ceasefire may end if no deal reached by Wednesday
Article:
MARYLAND: US President Donald Trump said on Friday that he may end the ceasefire with Iran unless a long-term deal to end the war is agreed by Wednesday.
"Maybe I won't extend it, but the blockade (on Iranian ports) is going to remain," Trump told reporters on Air Force One on his way back to Washington from Phoenix, Arizona.
"So you have a blockade, and unfortunately we have to start dropping bombs again." — REUTERS
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sultan Ismail Petra Airport can handle up to 4 million passengers a year - Loke
Article:
KOTA BARU: The Sultan Ismail Petra Airport (LTSIP) can accommodate up to four million passengers annually.
Transport Minister Anthony Loke described the airport as the most modern and aesthetically impressive domestic airport in the country.
He said the terminal size has been expanded from 12,000sqm to 36,000sqm.
In addition, he said parking facilities have been increased from 450 to 1,300 bays, enhancing convenience for users, while aircraft parking bays have been expanded from five to 11.
"The upgrading project for LTSIP is a manifestation of the Madani government's commitment to strengthening the nation's air transport infrastructure in an inclusive and sustainable manner, particularly in Kelantan.
"This effort is undertaken to meet the growing mobility needs of the people, while also stimulating local economic growth through increased tourism, trade and investment.
"This domestic airport is the most modern and aesthetically pleasing in the country as it has just been completed, and we hope it will be fully utilised to attract more tourists," he said.
He said this during his speech at the opening of the new terminal of Sultan Ismail Petra Airport here, which was officiated by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
Also present was Kelantan Menteri Besar Datuk Mohd Nassuruddin Daud.
He said that based on statistics from Malaysia Airports Holdings Bhd (MAHB), LTSIP handled more than 1.7 million passengers in 2025, an increase of 14.1 per cent from 1.47 million passengers in 2024.
He said the figure clearly shows that passenger volume has exceeded the terminal's original capacity of 1.5 million passengers annually by about 14.8 per cent, necessitating urgent government action to carry out upgrades to meet rising demand.
"This significant increase is driven by strong demand for domestic travel, particularly the strategic Kota Baru–Kuala Lumpur route, which is among the busiest in the country.
"In this regard, the federal government, through the Transport Ministry, has taken proactive steps by implementing the airport upgrading project to ensure service levels and operational capacity remain relevant to current needs.
"The RM440 million project is being carried out in phases, with Phase 1 completed and operational since May 2024.
"Phase 2 involves a more comprehensive scope of development, including full expansion of the passenger terminal and construction of a multi-storey car park," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sabah rice supply stabilises as state boosts production to 35,000 tonnes
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: Sabah's rice supply has stabilised following rigorous monitoring and an increase in production, with no further reports of retail shortages.
State Agriculture, Fisheries, and Food Industry Minister Datuk Jamawi Jaafar said the state's buffer stock is maintained at a level sufficient to last up to six months as a contingency against potential supply crises.
"We must have stock to last six months. This does not mean it will be exhausted at once; it is managed in stages to remain sufficient.
"If supply disruptions occur, such as exporting countries halting shipments, we have time to seek alternative sources without affecting the people," he said after a media session here yesterday.
Jamawi said engagements with Padiberas Nasional Bhd (Bernas) and wholesalers confirmed that the situation has improved.
"Current reports show the situation is stable and there are no more empty shelves," he said.
However, he revealed that distribution was being hampered by wholesalers failing to collect stock despite having made payments. Last week, approximately 3,000 tonnes of rice remained uncollected.
"This affects distribution even though stocks are sufficient. Various excuses were given, including rising diesel costs and a shortage of drivers, but firm action will be taken," he said.
He warned that regulatory authorities have been instructed to act against wholesalers found hoarding rice. Offenders face a minimum fine of RM50,000 and imprisonment.
To further secure the market, Jamawi said monthly production has been increased from the usual 20,000 metric tonnes to 35,000 metric tonnes this month.
He added that an audit of wholesalers is underway to identify those involved in stockpiling, with a report expected soon.
Jamawi also advised consumers to avoid panic buying, which could destabilise the market.
"There is no need to buy in excess, as rice can spoil if stored too long. Buy only according to your current needs," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Airlines hike fares as jet fuel prices surge amid Iran war
Article:
NEW YORK: A surge in jet fuel prices driven by the US-Israeli war on Iran has upended the global aviation industry, forcing airlines to raise fares and revise their financial outlooks.
Jet fuel prices have soared from US$85 to US$90 per barrel to US$150 to US$200 per barrel in recent weeks, a financial hit for an industry where fuel accounts for up to a quarter of operating expenses.
Below is a list of how airlines are responding, in alphabetical order:
AEGEAN AIRLINES
The Greek airline expects suspended Middle East flights and a spike in fuel prices to have a "notable impact" on its first-quarter results.
AIRASIA X
The Malaysian airline's executives said the company had cut 10 per cent of flights across the group, with a surcharge of about 20 per cent on fuel in general.
AIR CANADA
Canada's largest carrier plans to trim four of its 38 daily flights to New York due to higher fuel prices. The four flights to JFK International Airport will be cut from June 1 to Oct 25, 2026.
AIR FRANCE-KLM
The airline group said it planned to increase long-haul ticket prices to address surging fuel costs, with cabin fares set to rise by 50 euros (US$58) per round trip.
The group's Dutch arm KLM said on April 16 it would cancel 160 flights in Europe in the coming month due to rising fuel costs.
AIR INDIA
The Indian carrier said it would revise its fuel surcharge from a flat domestic surcharge to a distance-based grid. It said surcharges on international routes did not compensate for the exponential rise in fuel prices.
AIRLINE OPERATORS OF NIGERIA
The Nigerian industry body warned that Nigerian airlines would suspend all flight operations undefined from April 20 unless fuel prices are reduced, as it accused the country's fuel industry association of artificially raising prices in a letter seen by Reuters.
AIR NEW ZEALAND
The airline said on April 7 it would slash flights through May and June and hike fares, having been one of the first to announce broad increases to ticket prices when the conflict broke out. It also suspended its full-year earnings forecast due to fuel market volatility.
AKASA AIR
India's Akasa Air said it was introducing a fuel surcharge ranging between 199 and 1,300 Indian rupees (US$2 to US$14) on domestic and international flights.
ALASKA AIR
The US airline said it would increase fees for the first checked bag by US$5 and by US$10 for the second on its North American flights, as well as for its Hawaiian Airlines unit. It hiked prices for a third checked bag from US$50 to US$200.
AMERICAN AIRLINES
The US carrier said it would hike checked baggage fees by US$10 each for the first and second checked bags and by US$150 for the third checked bag on domestic and short-haul international flights. It also trimmed certain benefits for economy passengers.
It had earlier said it expected a US$400 million increase in first-quarter expenses due to fuel prices.
ASIANA AIRLINES
The South Korean airline will slash 22 flights between April and July due to the fuel cost increase, Newsis reported.
CATHAY PACIFIC
The Hong Kong airline said it would cut some flights from mid-May until the end of June, cancelling about 2 per cent of its scheduled passenger flights, while its budget airline HK Express was cutting around 6 per cent of flights.
The carrier previously said it would hike its fuel surcharge by 34 per cent across routes from April 1 and review them every two weeks.
CEBU AIR
The Philippines-based airline said the sharp rise in fuel prices was a key concern and it would continue to review its pricing and network strategies to mitigate the impact.
CHINA EASTERN AIRLINES
The airline said it would raise fuel surcharges for domestic flights from April 5, with flights of 800km and below hit with a 60 yuan (US$9) surcharge and a 120 yuan surcharge for flights over 800km.
DELTA AIR LINES
Delta said it would cut capacity by around 3.5 percentage points from its original plan and raise fees for checked bags in an attempt to offset soaring jet fuel costs, with an increase of US$10 on first and second checked bags and a US$50 increase on the third.
The US airline pulled all planned capacity growth for the current quarter and forecast profit below Wall Street expectations. Delta's CEO said it would hold off on updating the full-year outlook given uncertainty over how long the fuel price spike would last.
EASYJET
EasyJet warned of a bigger half-year pre-tax loss of between 540 million and 560 million pounds (US$731 million and US$758 million), including 25 million pounds in extra fuel costs in March.
CEO Kenton Jarvis previously said European consumers should expect higher ticket prices towards the end of summer, when existing fuel hedges come to an end.
FRONTIER AIRLINES
The US airline is reviewing its full-year forecast as fuel prices have increased significantly since it issued the outlook.
GREATER BAY AIRLINES
The Hong Kong-based company said it would raise fuel surcharges on most routes from April 1, while keeping them unchanged on mainland China and Japan routes.
Its surcharge for flights between Hong Kong and the Philippines will more than double, the carrier said.
HONG KONG AIRLINES
The airline said it would raise fuel surcharges by up to 35 per cent from March 12, with the sharpest increase on flights between Hong Kong and the Maldives, Bangladesh and Nepal, where charges would rise to HK$384 (US$49) from HK$284.
IAG
British Airways-owner IAG said in March it did not plan to increase ticket prices immediately, as it had hedged much of its fuel for the short- to medium-term.
INDIGO
India's biggest airline said it would introduce fuel charges on domestic and international flights from March 14, including a charge of 900 rupees for flights to the Middle East and a charge of 2,300 rupees for flights to Europe. The company is also lobbying the Indian government to cut fuel taxes, sources told Reuters.
JETBLUE AIRWAYS
The US-based low-cost carrier said it was increasing fees for optional services such as checked baggage as it experiences "rising operating costs." Baggage prices will rise by either US$4 or US$9, it said.
KOREAN AIR
The South Korean carrier will enter emergency management mode from April, as rising oil prices weigh on costs, a source with knowledge of the matter told Reuters. The airline plans to implement phased response measures based on oil price levels, and step up company-wide cost efficiency to offset surging fuel costs.
LUFTHANSA
The airline group said it would ground 27 planes servicing its short-haul CityLine subsidiary earlier than planned, citing jet fuel prices and costs from industrial action. Lufthansa will also withdraw four older Airbus A340-600 long-haul aircraft at the end of the summer and reduce short and medium-haul offerings by five aircraft in winter 2026/2027.
PAKISTAN INTERNATIONAL AIRLINES
The carrier said it would raise domestic flight fares by US$20 and international fares by up to US$100, citing higher fuel surcharges.
QANTAS AIRWAYS
Australia's Qantas said it had delayed a planned AUS$150 million (US$106 million) buyback and was raising its estimated fuel bill for the second half of 2026 to AUS$3.1 billion-AUS$3.3 billion, from a previous AUS$2.5 billion forecast.
SAS
The Scandinavian airline said it would cancel 1,000 flights in April because of high oil and jet fuel prices, after cancelling a "couple hundred" flights in March.
SAS, which had already increased flight prices, said that even if it tried to absorb the rising fuel costs, the price surge would still be a blow to the aviation industry.
SPRING AIRLINES
The budget Chinese airline said it would raise fuel surcharges on domestic flights from April 5, with details to be announced later.
SOUTHWEST AIRLINES
The American carrier said it would hike checked baggage fees by US$10 for the first and second bags, raising costs to US$45 for the first bag and US$55 for the second.
TAP
The Portuguese airline said its price hikes would partially mitigate the impact of fuel price changes on its revenue.
THAI AIRWAYS
The Thailand-based carrier said it would raise fares by 10 per cent to 15 per cent to address rising fuel costs.
TURKISH AIRLINES, LUFTHANSA
SunExpress, a joint venture between Turkish Airlines and Lufthansa, said it would impose a temporary fuel surcharge of 10 euros per passenger from May 1 on routes between Turkey and Europe. The surcharge will apply to bookings made on or after April 1 for departures on or after May 1.
Turkish Airlines said on April 10 it had decided not to distribute any dividend from its 2025 net profit, opting to retain earnings to preserve cash.
T'WAY AIR
The South Korean low-cost carrier said it planned to furlough some of its cabin crew without pay in May and June as part of measures to address the impact of the war.
UNITED AIRLINES
The US airline is cutting unprofitable flights over the next two quarters as it prepares for oil prices to remain above US$100 until the end of 2027, CEO Scott Kirby said.
United has been able to raise fares without materially hurting bookings in response to the rapid increase in oil and jet fuel prices, Chief Commercial Officer Andrew Nocella said.
It is also increasing first and second checked bag fees by US$10 for customers travelling in the US, Mexico and Canada and Latin America, it said in an e-mailed statement to Reuters.
VIETJET
The Vietnamese budget airline said it had adjusted flight frequency on selected routes due to potential fuel shortages.
VIETNAM AIRLINES
The carrier plans to cancel 23 flights per week across domestic routes from April, Vietnam's aviation authority said, after the airline requested government assistance to remove an environmental tax on jet fuel.
VIRGIN ATLANTIC
The airline is adding fuel surcharges to fares but will still struggle to return to profitability this year, its CEO Corneel Koster told the Financial Times.
VIRGIN AUSTRALIA
Virgin Australia said it expected an increase in jet fuel cost of around AUS$30 million-AUS$40 million for the second half of this fiscal year, and a 1 per cent reduction in capacity in the fourth quarter.
The airline previously said it was adjusting fares to reflect rising cost pressures.
WESTJET
The Canadian airline will add a C$60 (US$43) fuel surcharge to some bookings and combine flights as costs soar, the Canadian Press reported.--REUTERS
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Australia extends relaxed fuel standards to bolster supply
Article:
SYDNEY: Australia extended an easing on fuel-quality standards until September, Energy Minister Chris Bowen said on Saturday, as the nation grapples with the ​impact of the Iran war on its fuel supply.
"I've decided to extend the period of higher sulphur for petrol in Australia," Bowen said in televised remarks.
The relaxation, announced in March, increases the amount of sulphur permitted in fuel to 50 parts per million from the usual 10 parts.
Australia, which imports most of its fuel, has experienced localised shortages as the war, which entered its eighth week on Saturday, has disrupted supply chains.
Production of diesel, jet fuel and petrol at a fire-hit oil refinery owned by Viva Energy (VEA.AX) in Victoria, Australia's second-most-populous state, remained unchanged from Friday, Bowen said.
"The Geelong refinery is working at 80 per cent capacity for diesel and jet fuel and 60 per cent capacity for petrol — that is still the case," he said.
Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese said on Friday the fire would not trigger any fuel restrictions.
Also this week Albanese secured a deal with Malaysian state energy firm Petronas to supply excess ​fuel to Australia after trips to Singapore and Brunei aimed at strengthening energy supplies. — REUTERS
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Kosovo, Bosnia troops prepared for Gaza peacekeeping role
Article:
PRISTINA: Kosovo and Bosnia on Friday announced they were prepared to deploy small numbers of soldiers to Gaza as part of a peacekeeping force proposed by US President Donald Trump.
Kosovo's parliament unanimously approved a law authorising the deployment on Friday with media reports saying it intends to send 22 members of the security forces if the plan goes ahead, although the number has not been officially confirmed.
"The Republic of Kosovo proves that it is a reliable partner and ready to act alongside allies in support of peace, security, protection of civilians and implementation of international mandates," Defence Minister Ejup Maqedonci said ahead of the vote.
As for Bosnia, Defence Minister Zukan Helez has said more than 60 Bosnian soldiers are expected to join.
Bosnia's three-member presidency had previously approved the potential deployment in January.
Announced as part of a ceasefire agreement that halted two years of war, the planned International Stabilisation Force was proposed by the Trump-led "Board of Peace."
Kosovo joined several other countries in Washington for the Board's first meeting earlier this year to discuss funding for rebuilding Gaza and the potential deployment of thousands of foreign troops.
The proposed force could include up to 20,000 soldiers, with Indonesia so far pledging 8,000 troops, and would be under US command.
Kosovo's Prime Minister Albin Kurti said last month his country was willing to take part because of the role international forces, particularly Nato peacekeepers, played in his country after its 1998-1999 war of independence with Serbia.
The future of Trump's plan remains unclear, with Israel and Hamas maintaining contradictory demands in ongoing negotiations amid accusations of ceasefire breaches on both sides.
The ceasefire followed more than two years of war triggered by Hamas's Oct 7, 2023, cross-border attack on Israel.
At least 766 Palestinians have been killed since the October 10 ceasefire was announced, according to the Gaza Health Ministry, which is under Hamas authority and whose figures are considered reliable by the UN.--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PM prays for Malaysian haj pilgrims' ease, good health in Holy Land
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim has prayed for Malaysian haj pilgrims to be granted ease in their affairs and good health as they carry out their pilgrimage in the Holy Land.
He congratulated pilgrims who were selected to perform the haj this year and who began their journey early this morning.
"Alhamdulillah, my highest gratitude and congratulations to Malaysian haj pilgrims who have been chosen to answer the sacred call to Baitullah this year and have begun their journey to the Holy Land," he said in a Facebook post.
He said the haj is a journey that requires sacrifice, sincerity, patience and piety.
"I pray that all Malaysian haj pilgrims will have their affairs eased, be blessed with good health, and be protected throughout their time in the Holy Land.
"May all pilgrims be granted a mabrur haj, whose reward is none other than paradise, and return home with blessings, renewed awareness and a stronger resolve to be sincere servants of Allah," he said.
He also wished pilgrims well in performing their haj and prayed that all acts of worship would be accepted and blessed.
A total of 31,600 Malaysian haj pilgrims will perform the pilgrimage this year, with the
first group of 284
departing from Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) Terminal 1 at 4.05am today on a Malaysia Airlines flight, MH8050.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Injured student in SMK Tanjong Mas car crash moved to regular ward
Article:
KOTA BARU: The 13-year-old girl who was seriously injured after being struck by a car driven by a teacher at SMK Tanjong Mas has been moved to a regular ward.
State health director Datuk Dr Mohd Azman Yacob said the victim, Zara Aftar Zamzuri, is scheduled to undergo a corrective procedure on her ear this Monday.
"Alhamdulillah, the student was transferred to a regular ward following surgery last week. Our next step is a skin graft or repair on her ear scheduled for Monday," he said.
However, he noted that Zara is expected to remain hospitalised for an extended period to monitor her recovery and prevent infections.
Dr Mohd Azman spoke to reporters at the inauguration of the new Sultan Ismail Petra Airport (LTSIP) terminal here today.
He added that Psychological First Aid (PFA) has been initiated to support the teenager's mental recovery.
"We have started PFA because this was a sudden, traumatic event. Our psychiatry specialists will work to ensure her emotional state stabilises," he said.
In the incident last Sunday, Form One student Nur Fatimatul Hawa Mohd Azaudin, 13, was killed, while Zara was left in critical condition after being hit in front of the school's surau.
District police chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Azmir Damiri previously said the 1.50pm accident involved a female teacher who reportedly had accidentally stepped on the accelerator.
The car veered out of control, hitting the two students. One died at the scene from severe head injuries, while the other was rushed to the hospital for treatment.
Kelantan police chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat had said
that the teacher would be
charged in court under Section 41(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987 once investigations are completed.
She was released on police bail on Tuesday following the expiry of her remand order.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police: Losses from investment scams hit RM1.47 bln last year
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Investment scams caused losses of RM1.47 billion last year, involving 9,603 cases, with the Royal Malaysia Police identifying five main methods, most using social media as the primary channel.
Bukit Aman Commercial Crime Investigation Department director Datuk Rusdi Mohd Isa said tactics uncovered included fake companies (clone firms), promises of quick high returns, Ponzi schemes, love scams, and bogus crypto platforms.
He said syndicates lure victims via Facebook and Instagram, switch to WhatsApp or Telegram, then add them to fake investment groups run by supposed "professional traders."
"Victims see profits on fake platforms but can't withdraw funds, and are then asked to pay extra processing fees, taxes or other charges," he told Bernama.
He said alongside social media and messaging apps, trends now show growing use of crypto like USDT (Tether), with victims told to send funds to syndicate digital wallets via illicit platforms.
"In Ponzi schemes, initial profits come from new investors' money, until no more join and it all collapses," he said.
Rusdi said non-existent investment cases rose sharply from 2024 to March this year, with 6,337 cases causing RM848.6 million losses in 2024, surging to 9,603 cases and RM1.47 billion in 2025.
"Between January and March this year, police recorded 2,204 cases with losses of RM246.7 million, proof this crime is ongoing and worrying," he said, adding that stock investments topped the number of cases, followed by crypto and gold, showing the syndicate's trend of exploiting popular investment options.
"Victim profiles show those aged 31 to 50 are most affected, especially the 41-50 group.
"This is followed by ages 51-60 and 21-30, showing working-age groups active in finance and investments are most vulnerable," he said.
Rusdi said private sector workers ranked highest among victims, followed by civil servants and the self-employed.
He also urged the public not to be easily swayed by investment offers promising high returns in a short time, and to conduct checks before investing.
"Members of the public are also reminded not to make any payments to suspicious accounts, including those belonging to unregistered individuals," he added. — BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MIC still ‘core’ member of BN, says Zahid
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, April 18 — Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi today asserted that MIC is still a component member of the coalition, amid speculations over its status.
He added that while there had been communication on the matter, the party itself has denied any intention to leave BN.
“They are still core members of BN,” he told reporters when asked about MIC’s status after officiating at the AGM of BN ally, Malaysian Indian Muslim Congress at the World Trade Centre here.
“They didn’t request to leave us formally. There was apparently a letter sent to Perikatan Nasional to join them, but at the same time they denied that they had applied to leave BN,” he said.
MIC, alongside Umno and MCA, founded the Alliance that led Malaya in the early days of independence from colonial British rule, and remained on board when the coalition underwent a name change and expansion in 1973.
At its assembly last November, MIC delegates voted to leave BN.
But MIC president Tan Sri SA Vigneswaran later said the three motions in favour of exiting BN was deferred.

Title: Sudden lane change suspected in fatal Miri crash involving sports coupe and lorry
Article:
MIRI, April 18 — A sudden lane change is believed to have caused a fatal accident along Jalan Miri Bypass yesterday morning, said Miri District police chief ACP Mohd Farhan Lee Abdullah.
He said the accident involved a two-door sports coupe driven by a 40-year-old local man and a lorry driven by a 34-year-old local man.
The sports coupe, which was travelling from Permyjaya towards Miri Airport, is suspected to have suddenly changed lanes from the right to the left side of the road.
“As a result, the rear part of the car collided with the front part of the lorry, causing both vehicles to catch fire,” he said in a statement late Friday.
The car driver, who was trapped in the vehicle and engulfed in flames, was confirmed dead at the scene while the lorry driver was uninjured.
He said the case is now being investigated under Section 41(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987.
He urged members of the public with information on the incident to contact Traffic Investigating Officer Insp Mohd Faizal Moktar at 012-818 6509 or 085-430 479, or to visit the nearest police station.
He also advised road users to practise prudent driving and comply with all traffic regulations to avoid endangering others. — The Borneo Post

Title: Fahmi: Govt to streamline anti-scam efforts with new laws, joint agency session soon
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, April 18 — A special session to update all relevant stakeholders on current developments related to online scams will be held the soonest, Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil had said today.
He said this was a decision made during last week’s engagement meeting he chaired with ministry representatives, telecommunication companies and banks.
“We have been working closely with the relevant stakeholders on any matters relating to scams and in fact our last meeting was held last week.
“At the meeting we have decided that we need a separate engagement session with all those who are involved in tackling crimes relating to these online scams that today appear in various forms.
“We decided on this as we’ve noticed that each enforcement agency may not have the same understanding or knowledge on the matter and it is important that we are all on the same page on this matter,” Fahmi told reporters after officiating at a health campaign organised by Universiti Malaya at its campus here.
This is a proactive step taken by the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission to invite all relevant stakeholders for this separate session to ensure that all agencies who can contribute to combating these types of scams will have a similar understanding on the matter, he added.
“It isn’t just fraud, but all kinds of online scams be it investment scams, love scams and many various other types of scams that has been abusing official channels.
“So it’s not necessarily just focused on online scams that have been appearing via social media platforms, at times these scams appear on WhatsApp or Telegram, those are messaging systems.
“So hopefully in the nearest time we can hold this session to look at possible laws that could be created in relation to these online scams, or to look at ways we can streamline enforcement,” he said.
Fahmi added that the session would most likely be held in a few weeks’ time.

Title: Zahid backs Kimma entry into BN, but says other components must agree ‘100pc’ first
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, April 18 — Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi today said he is open to accepting the Malaysian Indian Muslim Congress (Kimma) as a full member.
At the same time, he said the party needs to first obtain unanimous approval from BN’s other  component parties.
“Umno has no issue. We have never questioned Kimma’s loyalty to Umno or BN,” he said at Kimma’s 46th annual general meeting, held in conjunction with its 50th anniversary at the World Trade Centre here.
“But the BN constitution is clear, any application to join as a component party must receive 100 per cent agreement from all existing parties,” he added.
He said Kimma supporters need to prove themselves to the BN supreme council and make a formal application.
He said the BN must present a cohesive front.
Zahid’s remarks come amid Kimma’s repeated bids to be admitted into BN over the years.
While several BN leaders have publicly indicated there is no objection to Kimma joining, the party has yet to secure unanimous backing required under BN’s constitution — a hurdle Zahid suggested could be resolved if there is genuine political will.
“We cannot say one thing in the media and another behind closed doors. That is not loyalty.
“Not bring someone in now and then change the rules later, that is flip-flopping. We must be consistent and suggest to the council to amend the constitution and we can move forward,” he said.
“We cannot say one thing in the media and another behind closed doors. That is not loyalty.
“I will propose that Kimma be accepted as a full member of BN, but it must be confirmed in the supreme council,” he said.
Zahid described his personal support for Kimma as rooted in loyalty and mutual respect.
“Trust me, I am your brother. We have never betrayed Kimma.
“Loyalty has a price, and it must be paid, no matter how expensive,” he said.
He added that while political ambitions are natural, they must be guided by sincerity and a sense of camaraderie.
“Loyalty must be accompanied by sincerity. Without sincerity, loyalty has no meaning,” he said.
“Do not be overwhelmed by power. Do not betray. If there is betrayal in the heart, there will be consequences.”
Zahid also cautioned against allowing narrow political interests to override broader national priorities, saying such actions could undermine unity within the coalition.
“When the interests of small groups override national interests, decisions will no longer reflect what should be done,” he said.
“Using religion for political gain is betrayal. We must avoid that.”
Zahid also assured Kimma delegates that all that they wanted would be considered, even though some might not be fulfilled immediately.
“Of the eight resolutions presented, maybe not 100 per cent can be implemented.
“But insyaAllah, a big part of it can be realised. That is what sincerity and friendship mean,” he said.
On the broader political landscape, Zahid said BN must capitalise on what he described as a growing “wave of support” from the public, but cautioned against relying purely on sentiment.
“We see the momentum growing, but we must study it properly.
“Is it real support, or just emotion? Can it be translated into votes? We must go down to the ground and engage the people properly,” he said.
He added that BN and Umno must continue strengthening grassroots ties, including with parties like Kimma, to rebuild dominance.
“People are longing for BN to be dominant again,” he said.
“We must not be afraid. We must continue working with the people.”
Zahid also called on coalition partners to work together in good faith, without suspicion or opportunism.
“To others, do not feel threatened. Work with us. Be loyal, be sincere, do not betray.
“If you only come when you need something and leave when you are comfortable, that is not friendship, that is opportunism,” he said.
Closing his speech, Zahid urged party leaders to embrace unity and bring in younger leaders to secure the coalition’s future.
“We must give opportunities to the youth. They are the majority of voters and the future of this country.
